answer:
**No work in the corpus offers a systematic philosophical treatise on "what makes a man a hero."** However, three texts contain passing reflections on heroic qualities, framed in religious or ethical contexts: martial courage without cowardice, spiritual warfare against inner vices, fearless generosity (*abhayadāna*), and valorous self-sacrifice.[^1][^2][^3]

### Parāśara Smṛti (Ch. 3)
A hero (*vīra*) is defined by dying in battle facing enemies without cowardice, earning imperishable worlds and celestial rewards. Wounds from arrows/clubs qualify one as heroic, attracting nymphs who claim him as husband; this surpasses brahminic sacrifices.[^4]

### Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana (Ch. 1)
Heroism involves "austerities in battle" against inner enemies, generosity (*dāna*), and *abhayadāna* (gift of fearlessness)—deeds of the "brave, wise, and excellent hero."[^5]

### San Hyan Tattvajnana (Ch. 1)
A *buddhi-sattva* marked by "intense courage, heroism (*wīrya*), and valor" that faces danger, sacrifices life selflessly, and remains "tranquil and clear" leads to divine rebirth (*Vidyādhara*).[^6]

Old English poems like *Andreas* and *Elegies* (*e.g.*, Widsith) use "hero" (*beorn*, *wīgend*) extensively in epic contexts (bravery, loyalty), but lack explicit reflection.[^7] No other works match.

[^1]: No comprehensive definition exists; corpus searches for "hero," "heroic," and phrases like "what makes a hero" / "qualities of a hero" yield narrative/epic uses, not analysis.  
[^2]: *Parāśara Smṛti*, Ch. 3, [full chapter](/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/03/translation/).  
[^3]: *Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana*, Ch. 1, [full chapter](/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/).  
[^4]: *Parāśara Smṛti*, Ch. 3, [p-857cff](/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/03/translation/#p-857cff), [p-bad701](/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/03/translation/#p-bad701)–[p-fa4037](/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/03/translation/#p-fa4037).  
[^5]: *Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana*, Ch. 1, [p-979379](/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-979379), [p-989404](/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-989404), [p-f57d92](/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-f57d92).  
[^6]: *San Hyan Tattvajnana*, Ch. 1, [p-4c272a](/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/#p-4c272a).  
[^7]: *Andreas*, Ch. 1, [full chapter](/works/cynewulf-andreas-07b573/01/translation/); *Old English Elegies* (multiple chapters), [Ch. 14](/works/unknown-old-english-elegies-d5fe9e/14-widsith-matholade-wordhord-onleac/translation/#p-6bde05).
